countries.name: Kenya
countries.summary: Stable operating environment with localized protest and crime concerns.
countries.overview: Kenya remains the regional commercial hub of East Africa, with deep capital markets, a stable currency regime, and a maturing regulatory framework. The country balances strong private-sector activity against pockets of political volatility, urban crime, and periodic protest activity tied to cost-of-living pressures and reform debates.
key_risks: Politically motivated protests and occasional civil disturbance, particularly in central Nairobi and major coastal centres
Persistent urban crime — opportunistic theft, carjacking risk in specific corridors, residential burglary
Localized terrorism exposure along the north-eastern border with Somalia
Heightened cyber-enabled fraud targeting financial services and telco channels
countries.outlook: Operating conditions are expected to remain broadly stable through 2026, with episodic political tension around fiscal policy. Devolution-era county dynamics continue to shape sub-national operating context. Investors should expect continued business but plan for occasional disruption windows.
recommendations: Maintain detailed journey management protocols for travel outside Nairobi metropolitan area
Subscribe to a daily monitoring brief for early warning on protest activity
Conduct refreshed fraud awareness training across customer-facing functions
Vet new staff and vendors with appropriate depth — verification fluency in local registries matters

countries.name: Uganda
countries.summary: Stable security environment with controlled political space and pockets of insurgency risk in the west.
countries.overview: Uganda offers stable macro conditions, a growing energy sector tied to oil development, and a generally permissive operating environment for established businesses. Political space remains controlled, and security tensions persist along the western border with the eastern DRC where ADF activity periodically affects civilian areas.
key_risks: Insurgency activity in western border areas (ADF spillover from DRC)
Politically motivated restrictions on dissent and civil society — operational implications for NGOs
Urban crime in Kampala — opportunistic and residential
Currency and economic management volatility tied to oil sector development cycles
countries.outlook: Stable operating environment through 2026, with elevated attention on the western border corridor and the political environment ahead of any electoral cycle. Energy-sector investment activity will continue to drive operational risk patterns.
recommendations: Route security plans for any movement to or through western border regions
NGO operators should maintain robust compliance and political-risk monitoring
Cash-handling and cash-in-transit risk warrants careful programme design
Engage with embedded advisory for sustained presence in higher-risk districts

countries.name: Tanzania
countries.summary: One of the more stable operating environments in East Africa, with steady economic growth.
countries.overview: Tanzania offers one of East Africa's more stable security environments and a steadily growing economy, supported by infrastructure investment and a generally pragmatic policy stance under current leadership. The Zanzibar archipelago carries its own political and security dynamics distinct from the mainland.
key_risks: Low-level criminality in urban centres — opportunistic theft and residential exposure
Occasional political tension on Zanzibar around electoral periods
Periodic regulatory uncertainty affecting specific sectors (mining, telecommunications)
Cross-border movement and customs friction with neighbours
countries.outlook: Stable conditions are expected to continue through 2026, with operational risk concentrated around regulatory navigation rather than direct security threat. Tourism and infrastructure sectors will remain growth-oriented.
recommendations: Build regulatory engagement into ongoing operational planning
Standard urban security precautions for residential and operational personnel
Monitor Zanzibar-specific dynamics separately during electoral windows

countries.name: Rwanda
countries.summary: Exceptionally low day-to-day security risk; tightly managed political space.
countries.overview: Rwanda combines exceptionally low day-to-day security risk with a highly organized state, strong regulatory predictability, and continued growth orientation. Political space is tightly controlled, and external dynamics — particularly Rwanda's relationship with the eastern DRC — shape the broader regional risk picture.
key_risks: Tightly managed political environment — operational implications for civil society and media-facing engagements
Regional security tensions tied to eastern DRC dynamics
Concentrated decision-making affects regulatory predictability at the margin
Limited but non-zero risk around the border with the DRC
countries.outlook: Stable, predictable operating environment expected through 2026. Continued attention to regional dynamics is warranted, particularly for organizations with cross-border operations.
recommendations: Standard security precautions are sufficient for most commercial operations
NGOs and media-facing organizations should engage thoughtfully on operational scope and messaging
Cross-border movement planning should account for evolving DRC dynamics

countries.name: Ethiopia
countries.summary: Active conflict in several regions; complex political transition continuing.
countries.overview: Ethiopia continues to navigate a complex political transition, with active conflict and humanitarian concerns in multiple regions — including Amhara, Oromia, and parts of the Tigray border zone. Economic reform efforts, including currency liberalization, are underway but bring their own adjustment pressures. The operating context demands engaged, intelligence-led decision-making.
key_risks: Active conflict and security incidents in Amhara, parts of Oromia, and border zones
Communal and ethnic tensions affecting specific regional operating contexts
Currency and macroeconomic volatility through ongoing reform implementation
Travel security risk extending well beyond Addis Ababa metropolitan area
Humanitarian access constraints in conflict-affected regions
countries.outlook: The 2026 operating environment is expected to remain elevated, with conflict dynamics, reform pressures, and political negotiation continuing to shape risk. Substantive in-country presence requires structured travel security, robust monitoring, and pre-positioned crisis response.
recommendations: Structured travel security and journey management for all movement outside Addis Ababa
Daily monitoring briefs with explicit regional coverage
Pre-positioned evacuation planning and crisis response capability
Vetting and partner diligence calibrated to operating region
Embedded advisory recommended for sustained substantive presence

countries.name: Nigeria
countries.summary: Concentrated security risks across multiple regions; major commercial market.
countries.overview: Nigeria remains West Africa's largest economy and a major commercial market, but operations there require carefully calibrated security and risk programmes. Risk varies sharply by region — from the established commercial environment of Lagos to active insurgency in the north-east, banditry in the north-west and middle belt, and kidnap-for-ransom across much of the country.
key_risks: Active Boko Haram and ISWAP insurgency in the north-east
Banditry, kidnap-for-ransom across the north-west and middle belt
Niger Delta militancy and energy-sector security risk
Urban crime and kidnap risk in major cities including Lagos and Abuja
Communal and inter-ethnic conflict in specific regions
Cyber-enabled financial fraud at scale
countries.outlook: Risk levels are expected to remain elevated through 2026, with persistent kidnap-for-ransom dynamics and continued insurgency activity in the north. Lagos and Abuja remain commercially viable with appropriate security programmes; substantive operations elsewhere require structured risk management.
recommendations: Comprehensive corporate security programmes for all permanent presence
Mandatory journey management for travel outside major commercial centres
Executive protection programmes for principals where threat assessment indicates
Subscriber-grade intelligence support including situational risk analysis
Pre-tested evacuation and crisis response capability

countries.name: Ghana
countries.summary: Among the more stable operating environments in West Africa; macroeconomic pressures notable.
countries.overview: Ghana remains among the most stable operating environments in West Africa, with a strong democratic tradition, peaceful electoral transitions, and a relatively open commercial environment. The country has navigated significant macroeconomic pressures and an IMF programme, with ongoing fiscal adjustment shaping the near-term context.
key_risks: Macroeconomic pressures, fiscal adjustment, and currency volatility
Localized criminality in urban centres
Northern border insecurity spillover from Sahel dynamics
Periodic protest activity tied to economic conditions
Sector-specific regulatory adjustments as part of reform programmes
countries.outlook: Operating environment is expected to remain stable through 2026, with macro and fiscal dynamics shaping commercial decisions more than direct security risk. Northern regions warrant heightened attention as Sahel security dynamics evolve.
recommendations: Standard corporate security programmes for urban and commercial centres
Heightened journey management for travel to northern regions
Macro and fiscal monitoring as part of operational planning
Vetting and due diligence calibrated to the regulatory adjustment context

countries.name: South Africa
countries.summary: Major economy with significant violent crime, infrastructure, and political risk dynamics.
countries.overview: South Africa combines Africa's most developed financial and commercial infrastructure with significant security and operational risks. Violent crime, infrastructure failures (power, water), labour-relations dynamics, and a complex political environment all shape the operating picture. Investment and operating activity remain substantial — but require well-designed security and continuity programmes.
key_risks: Violent crime including residential burglary, vehicle hijacking, and armed robbery
Major infrastructure failures, particularly electricity supply (load-shedding cycles)
Cash-in-transit and supply chain attacks at scale
Periodic civil unrest tied to economic conditions and political flashpoints
Labour-relations volatility in specific sectors
Cyber-enabled financial crime and corporate fraud
countries.outlook: Conditions are expected to remain elevated through 2026, with continued infrastructure pressures and persistent crime dynamics. Major commercial centres remain operationally viable with structured security programmes. Reform momentum could shift the medium-term picture.
recommendations: Comprehensive residential, executive, and corporate security programmes
Layered protection for supply chain and cash-handling operations
Infrastructure resilience built into business continuity planning
Vetting and process-control diligence calibrated to local risk patterns
Subscriber-grade intelligence covering security and macro dynamics

countries.name: Egypt
countries.summary: Stable security in major centres; macroeconomic and currency pressures notable.
countries.overview: Egypt offers stable security in major commercial centres, with controlled political space and active state security capacity. The macroeconomic environment remains under pressure, with currency adjustments, inflation, and continued IMF engagement shaping the operating context. The Sinai retains a distinct, higher-risk security profile.
key_risks: Macroeconomic and currency pressure; inflation impact on operating costs
Higher security risk profile in the Sinai peninsula
Controlled political environment with implications for civil society and media
Bureaucratic and regulatory complexity for new market entry
Periodic regional security spillover risk tied to broader Middle East dynamics
countries.outlook: Stable operating conditions in commercial centres are expected to continue, with macroeconomic adjustment pressures remaining the dominant operational concern through 2026. Sinai operations require structured security planning.
recommendations: Standard urban security programmes for Cairo and Alexandria operations
Structured security planning for any Sinai or border operations
Macro and currency monitoring integrated into commercial planning
Engaged regulatory navigation for new market entry or expansion

countries.name: DRC
countries.summary: Active conflict in eastern provinces; significant commercial activity continues despite extreme risk.
countries.overview: The Democratic Republic of Congo presents one of the most challenging operating environments on the continent. Active conflict in the eastern provinces — including North Kivu, South Kivu, and Ituri — continues to displace populations and generate humanitarian crisis. The mining sector remains commercially significant, but operations there require structured, sustained security and risk management.
key_risks: Active armed conflict in North Kivu, South Kivu, Ituri (M23, ADF, multiple armed groups)
Kidnap-for-ransom and personal security risk extending across multiple regions
Significant humanitarian crisis with civilian displacement and access constraints
Limited state security presence in conflict-affected regions
Regulatory and operational complexity in the mining and resource sectors
Communicable disease outbreaks periodically affecting specific regions
countries.outlook: The 2026 picture is expected to remain extremely challenging in eastern regions, with significant risk persisting throughout the year. Kinshasa and other major commercial centres remain operationally viable with strong programmes; substantive eastern operations require embedded security capability.
recommendations: Embedded security and intelligence capability for any substantive eastern operations
Comprehensive executive protection and journey management programmes
Pre-positioned evacuation capability and 24/7 crisis response
Vetting and partner diligence calibrated to extreme-risk operating context
Daily monitoring briefs with explicit regional coverage
